rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Ritual / practice (as noun) / ceremony — hard ban.
No stale markers, no 'fixed' retrospectives, no session biography.

rules_evaluated.evidence: line 21: "you know what the added coordination is buying you" — peer voice, no lecture register

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Don't smuggle terms of art from maintainer into body.
Punchy framing keeps its punch; carve-out lives downstream.
Value-prop / positioning framing belongs in sales copy + project-level strategy docs, NOT in student-facing curriculum body.
rules_evaluated.evidence: line 9: "Not toy demos. Working practitioners" — pedagogical framing (calibrate ceiling), not positioning defense. Register is teach-bound.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Watch out for planting jargon too early.
rules_evaluated.evidence: line 9: "Claude Code and OpenClaw" appear in prework opener without operational primers. Parenthetical defines relationship, not operation. Student hasn't seen either tool run yet.
rules_evaluated.fix: Add one sentence explaining operationally what Claude Code and OpenClaw each DO (not what they are) before naming them in the list context at line 11. Example: 'Claude Code is Anthropic's official agent-building interface you've been using; OpenClaw is the open-source equivalent that practitioners compare side-by-side. Both let you write and run multi-step agents.' Then the list at line 11 lands as names of people using those two tools.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Earn every technical term on first use.
rules_evaluated.evidence: line 27: 'subagent' earned in next sentence as 'helper the main agent dispatches'
line 30: 'own context window' — runtime-state usage, correct

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Reflection beats stay invisible in the flow.

summary: MAINTAINER_CUT: line 47. FENCE_RANGES: inline rt-code/rt-cowork spans (lines 23–25, 41). VERDICT: REVISE due to one blocking violation. FINDING: check_writing.md rule 14 / check_student_facing.md rule 2 — 'Claude Code' and 'OpenClaw' named on line 9 without operational primers. Student hasn't seen either tool run yet; the training context makes this a jargon-plant moment. FIX: Add one sentence explaining operationally what each tool does (not what it is) before the list at line 11.
